source_book: DC Pandey Chapter 5.2 (split from vector_basics bundle — see Example 5.5)

real_world_anchor.primary: A compass needle. It tells you which way north is — not how far north. It has no 'length' in the geographic sense. Magnitude is always 1 unit of pure direction.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Google Maps shows you an arrow pointing along your route. The arrow's direction is what matters — its length on your screen is not the distance to your destination.
real_world_anchor.tertiary: Rotate a torch beam around the room. The direction of the beam is a unit vector — the light travels in that direction regardless of brightness.

constraints: |A_hat| must equal 1 for every non-zero A
Unit vector of zero vector is undefined (division by zero)
Unit vectors carry no physical units — the division cancels dimensions
î, ĵ, k̂ are the canonical unit vectors along x, y, z axes

STATE_1.title: A Direction Without a Length — What Does That Even Mean?

scene_composition.text:
An arrow carries TWO things:
how much (length)
and which way (angle).
Can we strip one away?

tts_sentences.text_en: Every vector carries two pieces of information: how much, and which way.
The length is the magnitude. The tilt is the direction.
Now a strange question — can we keep only the direction and throw away the length?
Physics says yes. And the tool it uses is called a unit vector.

STATE_2.title: Divide the Vector by Its Own Length — Watch What Happens

scene_composition.text:
Â = A / |A|
Magnitude exactly 1.
Same direction as A.

tts_sentences.text_en: Take any vector A. Measure its length. Call that length the magnitude, written as A in bars.
Now divide the whole vector by that number.
The result is a new arrow pointing the same way, but with length exactly one.
We call it A-hat. The formula is A-hat equals A divided by the magnitude of A.
This hat is a direction extractor. It has thrown the length away and kept only the way it points.

STATE_3.title: î, ĵ, k̂ — The Three Canonical Direction Extractors

scene_composition.text:
Every vector in 3D
is built from these three.
A = Ax î + Ay ĵ + Az k̂

tts_sentences.text_en: Physics fixes three unit vectors as the standard reference directions.
i-hat points along the x-axis. j-hat points along the y-axis. k-hat points along the z-axis, into the page.
Each has length one. Each points along exactly one axis.
Any vector in three dimensions is just a weighted sum of these three. That is what it means when you write A equals Ax i-hat plus Ay j-hat plus Az k-hat.

STATE_4.title: Compute It Yourself — Â for A = 3î + 4ĵ

tts_sentences.text_en: Let us do one by hand. A equals three i-hat plus four j-hat — a 3 by 4 by 5 triangle.
The magnitude is square root of nine plus sixteen, which is five.
Divide every component by five. A-hat is zero point six i-hat plus zero point eight j-hat.
Always verify. Square the components, add them, take square root. You get one. That is the unit check — and you should never skip it.

epic_c_branches.misconception: Unit vector of A has the same magnitude as A, just written as components
trigger_phrases: unit vector of 10 newton force is 10 newton
A hat same magnitude as A

STATE_1.title: Show the Wrong Belief — Â With Length 10

scene_composition.text:
If Â had length 10,
then 10 × Â = 100 units.
That is not A.
The formula A = |A| × Â would break.

tts_sentences.text_en: Some students believe that A-hat has the same length as A.
If A is ten units long, they imagine A-hat also ten units long — just written in component form.
Watch what breaks. If A-hat has length ten, then ten times A-hat would be one hundred units. That is not the original A.
The whole formula A equals magnitude A times A-hat only works if A-hat has length exactly one.

STATE_2.title: The Division — Watch the Arrow Shrink

scene_composition.text:
Divide by 10.
Length collapses to 1.
Direction stays east.

tts_sentences.text_en: Start with A equals ten i-hat — ten units pointing east.
Now divide it by ten. Every component shrinks by a factor of ten.
The arrow that was ten units long becomes one unit long. Still pointing east. That is A-hat.
Division squeezed the magnitude down to one. It did not change the direction at all.

STATE_3.title: Why It HAS to Be One — The Proof

scene_composition.text:
Unit vectors also carry
NO physical units.
[N] / [N] cancels.
[m/s] / [m/s] cancels.

tts_sentences.text_en: The proof is one line. Magnitude of A-hat equals magnitude of A divided by magnitude of A — which is one.
Two more things fall out of this. First — the units cancel. If A is measured in newtons, the newtons in the numerator and denominator cancel. A-hat is dimensionless.
Second — i-hat, j-hat and k-hat have no units either. They are pure direction labels.
Source — DC Pandey Example five point five.
A equals six i-hat minus eight j-hat. Find A-hat.
Magnitude — square root of thirty six plus sixty four — is ten.
Divide each component by ten. Zero point six i-hat minus zero point eight j-hat.
Always finish with the check. Square the components. Add. Take root. You get one. Pattern holds.

regeneration_variants.label: Different numbers, same 3-4-5 triangle physics
regeneration_variants.physical_world: A = 5î + 12ĵ (5-12-13 triangle), A = 8î + 15ĵ (8-15-17 triangle). Same division process.

regeneration_variants.label: Force vector instead of geometric
regeneration_variants.physical_world: A 30 newton force at 60° above horizontal. Extract the direction as a unit vector, discard the 30 newton scale.
